Audit complete. Casa Rosario video is now synced across native Android, iOS, and the landing page.

**Root cause:** The video existed in the backend feed but the canonical home-feed required it to be attached to a real deal via `property_id`/`project_id`. The row had a UUID project_id that did not match the `jv_deals` slug `casa-rosario-001`, so it was silently excluded.

**What I fixed:**
- Backend: added title-based fallback matching in `handlePlatformHomeFeed`, so a video whose title contains a deal name attaches to that deal.
- Android `DealVideoCard`: added Tokenized / JV Deal / Buyer investment-option icon chips (mirrors iOS).
- iOS: already had title-based matching in `ProjectReelsViewModel`, so it synced automatically once the backend returned the video.
- Landing page: `ivx-home-feed.js` consumes the same endpoint, so it synced without redeploy.

**Live proof:**
- Backend deploy: `commit 1aa49ae5` live on `api.ivxholding.com`
- Home feed: `https://api.ivxholding.com/api/ivx/video-platform/home-feed?limit=60`
  - `video_count: 1`
  - `blocks: 4`
  - Video block at position 3: **Casa Rosario — Pembroke Pines, FL**
  - Attached deal: **Casa Rosario** (`casa-rosario-001`)

**Builds:**
- iOS: `runChecks` passed
- Backend: `runChecks` passed
- Expo TypeScript: `tsc --noEmit` clean

Note: `runChecks` for Expo reports a false-positive “backend/hono.ts missing” — the file exists and is deployed; the only real TypeScript error was fixed and pushed.
